title: Remove SPEC contract contradictions
description: Remove parts of SPEC.md that conflict with spec/OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md so the product spec delegates canonical on-chain behavior to the smart-contract spec.
close_reason: Removed SPEC.md content that contradicted OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md and delegated canonical on-chain mechanics to the smart-contract spec.

title: Recovery after partial ITM settlement
description:
## What to build

Allow admin recovery after partial ITM settlement where exercised and unexercised obligations were paid through mixed seller settlement.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Recovery accounts for mixed exercise proceeds and returned collateral before identifying excess balances.
- [ ] Recovery cannot withdraw anything reserved for unsettled seller vaults or active accounting obligations.
- [ ] Dust and excess recovery remains delayed, evented, and checked against collateral pool accounting.

## Blocked by

- Partial ITM settlement after missed exception

title: Recovery for claim-pool settlement path
description:
## What to build

Allow admin recovery for series settled through exercise-by-exception while preserving all claim pool balances owed to late long holders.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Recovery can clean up excess or dust in old series/collateral objects after claim-pool settlement is complete.
- [ ] Recovery cannot withdraw claim-pool balances reserved for unclaimed matching long tokens.
- [ ] Closed or depleted claim-pool cleanup is supported where storage can be safely reclaimed.

## Blocked by

- Exercise-by-exception and claim pools

title: Recovery after manual or no-exercise settlement
description:
## What to build

Allow admin recovery of excess balances and rounding dust after manual, fully exercised, ATM, or OTM settlement paths without touching active obligations.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Recovery is limited to balances not reserved for active, exercisable, or unsettled series obligations.
- [ ] Rounding dust is recoverable only after the configured conservative recovery delay.
- [ ] Recovery emits the required admin recovery event with asset, amount, recipient, and reason code.

## Blocked by

- Manual exercise and seller settlement

title: Partial ITM settlement after missed exception
description:
## What to build

Settle ITM series after the exception window when some longs were manually exercised but exercise-by-exception did not complete.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] After the exception window, remaining unexercised ITM longs are no longer exercisable or claimable when exercise-by-exception did not complete.
- [ ] Seller settlement pays the exercised portion from exercise proceeds and returns original collateral for the unexercised portion.
- [ ] Settlement remains permissionless, batched, pro-rata by seller short quantity, and dust-safe.

## Blocked by

- Manual exercise and seller settlement
- Exercise-by-exception and claim pools

title: Manual exercise and seller settlement
description:
## What to build

Implement holder-initiated manual physical exercise and permissionless seller settlement for no-exercise and fully exercised series.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Manual exercise consumes the whole matching long token, enforces the finalized ITM exercise window, validates payment, and transfers the correct physical settlement asset.
- [ ] Exercise accounting records quantities and proceeds for both calls and puts without overdrawing the collateral pool.
- [ ] Permissionless batched seller settlement pays sellers directly for ATM/OTM and fully exercised ITM outcomes, closes settled vault records, and closes the series when complete.

## Blocked by

- Call and put underwriting
- Price finalization and phase derivation

title: Price finalization and phase derivation
description:
## What to build

Finalize immutable expiry prices and expose deterministic phase and moneyness behavior for option series.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Price finalization is permissionless after expiry, validates oracle identity/feed id/publish time/positive price, and stores the price immutably.
- [ ] Batch finalization only accepts series from the same market.
- [ ] Derived phase and ITM/ATM/OTM behavior matches the spec for calls, puts, manual exercise, exception window, partial settlement, and full settlement.

## Blocked by

- Expiry price finalization adapter
- Series creation with collateral pool

title: Call and put underwriting
description:
## What to build

Support fully collateralized call and put underwriting that creates transferable long option tokens, records seller short obligations, and handles premium and fee settlement.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Call underwriting deposits BaseCoin collateral and put underwriting deposits QuoteCoin collateral using solvency-favoring strike payment rounding.
- [ ] Underwriting mints the correct long token to the buyer, updates one seller vault record per seller/series, and increases series short quantity.
- [ ] Premium, operational fee, fee recipient, and optional max-fee checks are enforced atomically.
- [ ] Long tokens support split and join only for identical option class and series fields.

## Blocked by

- Series creation with collateral pool

title: Market admin and pause lifecycle
description:
## What to build

Implement the shared Market lifecycle with admin authority, supported asset identity, and global pause behavior for the options protocol.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Markets store the OracleBase / QuoteCoin / BaseCoin combination, oracle metadata, decimals, strike scale, supported coin types, admin authority, and pause state.
- [ ] Unsupported coin types are rejected by market-scoped operations introduced in this slice.
- [ ] Pause and unpause require admin authority and emit the required events.

## Blocked by

- Sui Move project lean setup

title: Series creation with collateral pool
description:
## What to build

Allow anyone to create a valid option series for an existing market, with exactly one isolated collateral pool for that series.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Series creation rejects invalid option type, zero strike, past expiry, unsupported market coin types, and duplicate market/type/strike/expiry tuples.
- [ ] Each created series references exactly one collateral pool for that series and emits series creation data.
- [ ] Series state and timing fields support the spec's derived expiry phases without adding extra stored phase states.

## Blocked by

- Market admin and pause lifecycle

title: Expiry price finalization adapter
description:
## What to build

Decide and encode the contract-facing expiry price finalization adapter shape for permissionless option series finalization after expiry.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The accepted oracle source, proof/payload shape, and validation responsibilities are recorded clearly enough for implementation.
- [ ] The adapter design supports market oracle name/feed id checks, positive price checks, publish time at or after expiry, and batch finalization by market.
- [ ] The design leaves exercise blocked when no valid bounded expiry price can be finalized.

## Blocked by

- Sui Move project lean setup

title: Sui Move project lean setup
description:
## What to build

Create the lean Sui Move package foundation for the options smart contract work so later slices can add production behavior without reworking project structure.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The repository has a minimal Sui Move package configured for the options protocol.
- [ ] The package builds with the chosen Sui toolchain and contains only scaffold-level modules/tests needed by follow-on slices.
- [ ] The package metadata or local docs make the intended build/test command clear for AFK agents.

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately
notes: Implemented lean packages/options Sui Move scaffold with Move.toml, package_info source module, package_info test, and local README build/test commands. Validation attempted with sui 1.72.1 using writable HOME; build/test are blocked by restricted network while fetching github.com/MystenLabs/sui.git framework deps.
close_reason: Completed lean Sui Move package scaffold; sui move test and sui move build pass for packages/options.

title: Exercise-by-exception and claim pools
description:
## What to build

Implement permissionless exercise-by-exception for remaining ITM longs and create claim pools that late holders can redeem against by burning matching long tokens.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] During the exception window, anyone can settle the remaining unexercised ITM quantity for calls and puts when required seller payment and minimum net claim constraints are satisfied.
- [ ] Net proceeds for late holders are deposited into a claim pool only when non-zero, and the operation records exercise-by-exception accounting.
- [ ] Claim pool redemption burns a matching long token, pays a solvency-rounded pro-rata share, handles final rounding dust safely, and does not require loading old series or seller vault records.

## Blocked by

- Price finalization and phase derivation
- Flash-loan exercise composability

title: Flash-loan exercise composability
description:
## What to build

Make exercise paths composable inside Sui PTBs so callers can combine exercise proceeds with external flash-loan, swap, and repayment routes atomically.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Core exercise functions expose PTB-friendly return values or wrappers that allow proceeds to be routed before final transfer.
- [ ] The options contract does not hard-code any lending protocol, DEX, swap route, or provider-specific dependency.
- [ ] Tests or examples demonstrate that borrow/exercise/repay-style composition can be expressed atomically around the core exercise path.

## Blocked by

- Manual exercise and seller settlement
